known_pain_points: Small agency (founded 2021) — limited public review surface area (1 Clutch review)
No BBB profile or sustained Reddit/Trustpilot footprint to validate at scale
Project minimum $5K may price out very small single-bay shops
Not collision-repair-specialized — DRP, OEM certification, and CCC/CarWise nuances not core to their playbook

Ulmer's Auto Care — ReTread case study ($34,017 in 30 days, 4,306% campaign ROI)
Multi-location NC auto repair + tire dealer client cited on Clutch
pros: Founders (David Christopher, Neal Maier) are former shop owners — credible vertical expertise
Tire + AR vertical focus = better keyword/creative/seasonality playbooks than generalist SMB agencies (Hibu, Scorpion)
Proprietary DRIVE framework and ReTread re-engagement product with ROI guarantees
Strong published case-study results (276% leads, 4,306% campaign ROI)
Industry-active (Gain Traction Podcast, NTDA presence, tire-trade-press visibility)
Pricing partially transparent on Clutch ($5K project min, $150-$199/hr)
cons: Young agency (2021) with thin third-party review surface (1 Clutch review at last check)
Tire/AR vertical focus means little-to-no collision-repair playbook depth
$5K project floor likely above where Koffman currently spends on Hibu's AR-side allocation
Headquarters in Raleigh NC (not Houston as briefed) — verify before client conversations
Retainer length, cancellation, and asset ownership terms not public
koffman_relevance: Not relevant today for Koffman's collision-repair-focused business — Tread Partners' entire playbook is tire dealers and independent AR shops, not collision. Would NOT replace Hibu (current bundled SMB vendor) or Turnkey (collision-side) for the CR side of Koffman's operation. ONLY becomes relevant if Koffman expands the mechanical/tire/general-AR side of the business and wants a vertical specialist for that line — at which point Tread Partners (alongside AutoLeap Marketing, Autoshop Solutions, Shop Marketing Pros) is one of the top 3-4 AR-specialist agencies to evaluate. Their AR/tire expertise, ReTread program, and DRIVE framework would substantially outperform Hibu's generic SMB playbook for that segment.
